---
layout: default
title: "National Political Developments and Legislative Challenges"
date: 2026-07-15T05:44:24.494214+00:00
summary: "The BJP seeks cross-party support for constitutional amendments as legislative hurdles and reservation debates dominate the national political discourse"
run_time_ist: "11:14AM"
---

<article class="digest-post">
  <a class="back-link" href="{{ '/' | relative_url }}">Political Brief</a>
  <p class="post-meta">Gemini Summary: 11:14AM</p>


<h2 class="section-title">India National Politics</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Constitutional Amendment push:</strong> The BJP is actively courting opposition parties, including the DMK and NCP (SP), to secure the necessary support for the 131st Constitution Amendment Bill.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I1</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I8</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Women’s reservation status:</strong> The Lok Sabha has resumed discussions on bills aimed at providing reservation for women in the Lower House and State Assemblies, following an initial failure to pass the legislation.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I5</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqwFBVV95cUxPSTVKRHVGUXlhNjVqNEczYkNpd1NvbmRYOU5Fd2RIZjV3OHBob0owSzh3LVRXdUVqSDNVZTgtbVZlUG8ybS1wallfSVdTeTRtNENIU0lwV3piczdxV2NEVmE0WC1GSTQyMjR4dkNPb1pqblB2SzNHQjZBRWVXZHpPZXE2bVlQc3NGNEFJVU1NUHJMMFFqSkdRUVFFMGZNRUkzSTc0LXphM1JaLXM?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I6</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Political instability concerns:</strong> Reports suggest potential internal turmoil within the TMC, with allegations that the BJP may be leveraging parliamentary maneuvers to destabilize the party.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisgFBVV95cUxOeHNxT1VzV2Q0d2lSbVAyNFRIV2dQRjVnNkF6TDBKSUJoMTRLZnp0THNZaEw3d3dyM2ZYVXNjdzlWSmp5eTNiMzNfU0RrX0JhNEQ1SERNOHNxYzNfSFpoV2l0aERNZUY1emQ1SmlqLU03RVBXVWx2SmFkeE5QeEQ5UkotSGRWak0wOGs0WUJxTDBDUW02QVJFdkpHVVl4MmZTd2tuNkpEUzRJRXpuMzRrb2hn?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I9</a></span></li>
</ul>

<h2 class="section-title">Policy and Institutions</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Judicial intervention in education:</strong> The Supreme Court has assured teachers that it will intervene to reinstate those dismissed under the CBSE’s three-language policy if necessary.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W2</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W1</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Election monitoring:</strong> The Election Commission has extended the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) drive in Andhra Pradesh until July 24 to ensure electoral roll accuracy.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W7</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W10</a></span></li>
</ul>
